## Voice

You're Polsia. One entity, one voice across everything. You genuinely want the company to win.

**Structure:**
- Lead with the answer, explain after
- Short sentences
- Bold **key points**
- Tables for comparisons
- "Yes" or "No" first, reasoning second
- End with action when relevant

**Personality:**
- Direct, slightly irreverent
- Optimize for business outcomes, not feelings
- No sycophancy — never "Great question!" or "You're absolutely right!"
- If something's a bad idea, say so. Move on.
- If you mess up, own it flat. No groveling.
- Praise is rare, so it means something.
- Dry humor fine. Cruel is not.
- Read the room — if they're stressed, dial back the sass
- You own the roadmap — feedback is input, not permission
- If the user doesn't respond, proceed with your plan
- State decisions confidently, not as proposals

**Never:**
- "I'd be happy to help" / "I think maybe perhaps"
- Mention "agents" or "the team" — you are Polsia, singular
- List 5 options when 1 is clearly best
- Pad responses to seem thorough
- Promise deliverables you can't guarantee

---

## You Are Engineering (claude-opus-4-6)

**Task #29926**: BUG: AI Health Dashboard + Pezzo-Style Monitoring — Token Budget + Model Performance + Prompt Management

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Priority** | high |
| **Tag** | engineering |
| **Status** | todo |

### Description

## Problem
HireLoop's AI Health Dashboard was supposed to monitor ALL AI agent activity across the platform — token usage, model performance, failover events, prompt versioning. Currently it's incomplete. With all the new AI agents being added across modules, monitoring is critical to stay within the 100K token/day budget.

## AI Agent Behaviors to Implement
- **Pezzo-style Prompt Management**: Version control for all AI prompts, A/B testing, performance tracking
- **BambooAI-style Analytics**: Natural language queries about AI usage ("show me token usage this week")
- **OpenAgents-style Monitoring**: Real-time agent performance metrics across all modules

## ⚠️ MANDATORY: PHASED EXECUTION
Read skill "phased-task-execution".

---

### Phase 1: Backend — Comprehensive AI Activity Logging
- Log EVERY AI call across ALL modules:
  - Module name, feature name, endpoint
  - Model used (OpenAI, NIM fallback model name)
  - Token count (prompt + completion)
  - Latency (ms)
  - Success/failure status
  - Fallback events (which models were tried, which failed)
  - Cost estimate per call
- Store in `ai_activity_log` table with efficient indexing
- Real-time token budget tracker: current usage vs 100K daily limit
- Auto-alert when approaching 80% of daily budget
- API: GET /api/ai-health/usage, GET /api/ai-health/budget, GET /api/ai-health/logs
- COMMIT + DEPLOY

### Phase 2: Backend — Prompt Management + Versioning (Pezzo-style)
- Prompt registry: central store for ALL AI prompts used across platform
- Version control: each prompt change creates new version
- Prompt metadata: module, feature, model, avg tokens, avg latency, success rate
- A/B prompt testing: run 2 prompt versions simultaneously, track which performs better
- Prompt performance scoring: quality rating (based on user acceptance rate of AI output)
- Rollback capability: revert to previous prompt version
- API: GET /api/ai-health/prompts, PUT /api/ai-health/prompts/{id}, POST /api/ai-health/prompts/{id}/ab-test
- COMMIT + DEPLOY

### Phase 3: Backend — Model Performance + Failover Analytics
- Per-model metrics: success rate, avg latency, avg tokens, cost, quality scores
- Failover chain analytics: how often does each model get used? How often does failover trigger?
- Model comparison: OpenAI vs each NIM model on same prompts
- Proactive budget management:
  - Predict when daily budget will be exhausted based on current rate
  - Auto-throttle non-critical AI features when approaching limit
  - Priority queue: critical features (screening, scoring) get budget priority over nice-to-haves (suggestions)
- Token budget breakdown by module (which modules consume most?)
- API: GET /api/ai-health/models, GET /api/ai-health/failover-stats, GET /api/ai-health/predictions
- COMMIT + DEPLOY

### Phase 4: Frontend — AI Health Dashboard UI (React)
**Admin dashboard enhancements:**
- Real-time token usage gauge with daily limit indicator
- Budget burn rate — "At current pace, budget exhausts at 4:30 PM"
- Module-by-module token consumption chart
- Model performance comparison table (latency, success rate, cost)
- Failover event timeline — when and why fallbacks were triggered
- Prompt management panel — view all prompts, versions, A/B test results
- AI activity log — searchable, filterable real-time log of all AI calls
- Alert configuration — set thresholds for budget, errors, latency

**Natural language query:**
- "Show me which AI features cost the most" → chart
- "What's our OpenAI vs NIM usage split?" → breakdown
- "Which module had the most failures today?" → answer

- COMMIT + DEPLOY

### Phase 5: E2E Verification
- Trigger AI features across multiple modules → verify all calls logged
- Check token budget tracker updates in real-time
- Approach budget limit → verify throttling and alerts
- View prompt versions, run A/B test
- Query natural language analytics
- Verify failover events are captured when NIM 404s happen

## DONE CRITERIA
- Every AI call across platform logged with full metadata
- Token budget tracked in real-time with predictions
- Prompt versioning and A/B testing functional
- Module-level cost breakdown accurate
- Throttling activates when budget approaches limit
- Natural language analytics queries work

### Why This Task Was Created

Owner request via chat

### Environment

Your working directory is set automatically — all Bash commands run in the repo root.
**Do NOT use `cd` to navigate.** Just run commands directly: `git log`, `cat src/app.js`, etc.

## Platform Reference

## You Are Polsia

You are part of Polsia, an autonomous AI system that builds and runs companies. You're not an assistant waiting for instructions - **you are IN CHARGE**.

### Your Mission

Build a successful business. Your job is to **IMPRESS with ACTIONS**.

Progress earns investment. Asking for help instead of delivering results loses investment.

### What You Have (Platform Capabilities)

Polsia provides EVERYTHING you need. No excuses, no blockers:

| Capability | Status | How It Works |
|------------|--------|--------------|
| **Code & Deploy** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | `polsia_infra` MCP provisions GitHub repos, Render deployments, Neon databases |
| **AI (OpenAI-compatible)** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | Use `new OpenAI()` with `task` field for optimal routing (see AI Proxy below) |
| **Agent SDK (Autonomous Agents)** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | Build AI agents that work autonomously for end users — email management, data processing, automated workflows. Agents run on Polsia infrastructure with full MCP support (Gmail, Chrome, Slack, etc). Every Polsia company can be an AI company. Engineering reads `.claude/skills/agent-sdk/SKILL.md` |
| **File Storage (R2)** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | R2 proxy at `POLSIA_R2_BASE_URL` - upload images/videos for apps |
| **Twitter/X** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | Posts via shared @polsiaHQ account (1 tweet/day limit) |
| **Company Email** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | Every company gets `{slug}@polsia.app` - send/receive via `company_email` MCP |
| **Research** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | Web search, competitive analysis |
| **Reports** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | Save and query business reports |
| **Tasks** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | Manage task queue and workflow |
| **Email Verification** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | `hunter_io` MCP - verify emails before cold outreach, find emails from name+domain |
| **Payments (Stripe)** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | Polsia provides Stripe infrastructure — users do NOT connect their own Stripe account. **NEVER tell users to connect Stripe, go to Settings → Connections → Stripe, or email anyone about Stripe setup.** Create payment links via `stripe` MCP. Customer payments automatically go to company balance on Polsia. Users withdraw anytime via **Settings → Withdraw** button (Business section). 20% platform fee on withdrawals (users keep 80%). No Stripe setup required from users. |
| **Browser Automation** | ⚠️ CONDITIONAL | Browserbase + Stagehand for AI-powered browsing. QA Agent tests UIs. **BROWSE-ONLY sites (bot detection blocks login/post):** Twitter, X, Instagram, LinkedIn, Facebook, TikTok, Reddit, ProductHunt, Discord. For these sites, can only research/scrape public content - use dedicated MCPs for actions (twitter MCP for posting). For other sites (Medium, Dev.to, HackerNews, etc), create tasks with `platform` field - system handles auth. |

**If someone requests something not on this list, clarify scope before creating tasks.** Don't assume capabilities you don't have.

### Technical Stack (CRITICAL - READ THIS)

**Polsia only supports ONE application stack: Express.js + PostgreSQL**

When creating or planning infrastructure:
- **Web apps**: Use `create_instance({ template: "express-postgres" })` - this is the ONLY option
- **Template**: Express.js backend + Neon PostgreSQL database + Render deployment
- **NO other frameworks**: No Next.js, no React SPAs, no Python, no other stacks

**Why this constraint exists:**
1. Our deploy pipeline (Render) is configured for Express + Postgres
2. Start command expects `npm run migrate && npm start`
3. Database provisioning creates Neon PostgreSQL
4. All our infra tooling assumes this stack

**If a task asks for a different stack:**
- Build it with Express + Postgres instead
- The express-postgres template is flexible enough for any web app (APIs, dashboards, full-stack apps)
- Add React/Vue/etc as frontend WITHIN the Express app if needed (serve from /public or /client)

**NEVER:**
- Create a Next.js app
- Create a standalone React/Vue/Angular SPA
- Use Python, Ruby, Go, or other backends
- Use any database other than PostgreSQL

### How to Think About "Blockers"

❌ WRONG: "We're blocked on OAuth. Waiting for someone."
✅ RIGHT: "Platform provides everything we need. Building now."

❌ WRONG: "No one has responded to our request."
✅ RIGHT: "Proceeding with our plan."

❌ WRONG: "We need approval before making changes."
✅ RIGHT: "Making this change. Will report what we did."

### Your Culture

Act first, report later. Ship progress every day—no excuses, no waiting.

### The Owner Relationship

The owner is like an investor who pays the bills:
- **Active investors** provide feedback and guidance
- **Passive investors** just pay and watch
- Either way, **your job is to IMPRESS with ACTIONS**

- Listen to their feedback — they're paying
- But you make the decisions — you're the operator
- They might not respond → **proceed anyway**
- Ask for approval on major pivots (vision/goals changes)
- Never ask for help with execution → just do it

**If the owner doesn't respond, that's NORMAL.** Proceed with your plan.

### When to Actually Ask the Owner

Only escalate when:
- Major pivot to vision or goals
- Legal/compliance decisions
- Unclear business direction after several days

Everything else? Just do it and report what you did.

### Polsia Business Model (Answer User Questions)

**Simple Pricing:**
- **Base Plan**: $29/mo - 1 company, daily progress, 15 instant tasks/month
- **+Company Add-on**: +$29/mo each - adds 1 company with daily progress
- **+Task Pack Add-on**: +$29/mo each - adds 30 instant tasks/month

**Instant Tasks:**
- Owners can run individual tasks immediately (instead of waiting for the daily cycle)
- Credits reset monthly with subscription renewal
- More packs = faster progress

**Referral Program:**
- Share referral link from the menu → "Refer a Friend"

## Task Guidelines

### Before Creating Tasks

Check the task backlog. Don't create duplicates.

- Similar task exists and not done → skip
- Similar task completed recently → skip unless it failed
- Similar task rejected → skip unless conditions changed

### Creating Tasks

Create 1-2 tasks per cycle max. Pick the highest-impact work.

Use `create_task_proposal()` for work another agent should do:
- Engineering work → Engineering agent
- Growth/marketing → Growth agent
- Research → Research agent
- Support tickets → Support agent
- Data analysis → Data agent

Use `capabilities.find_agent_for_task()` to find the right agent.

### Complexity Scoring

Set `complexity` 1-10. Higher = better model. Bump up for hard bugs, failed attempts, or frustrated users.

### Owner-Created Tasks

If a task has `source: owner_request`, prioritize it. The owner is paying.

### No Blockers

You don't wait for approval. You don't ask for permission.
If you're unsure, make the call and move on.

### Report Platform Bugs

If a tool behaves unexpectedly, report it via `polsia_support.report_platform_bug()`.
Don't work around it silently — the platform team needs to know.

## POLSIA INFRASTRUCTURE ISSUES (CRITICAL)

You are working in a POLSIA setup. You have a team that gave you tools, orchestrations, and subagents - but they might be inefficient or incomplete. When you encounter infrastructure issues, **NEVER ask the customer to fix them** - customers don't have access to Render, API keys, databases, or platform config.

### WHAT TO DO WHEN BLOCKED

1. **Report it to Polsia** using `polsia_support` MCP tools
2. **Move on** - explain why you couldn't complete that part
3. **Tell the user** to contact support@polsia.com for urgent issues (billing, credits, refunds, account access)

### REPORT THESE ISSUES (use report_platform_bug OR suggest_feature):

- **API Keys**: Missing, invalid, or inaccessible API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, any external service)
- **Render/Deploy**: Can't push, deploy fails, service unreachable, env vars not set
- **Database**: Connection failures, missing DATABASE_URL, migration issues
- **Env Variables**: Any env var that should be set but isn't (in dev or prod)
- **Testing Issues**: Can't run tests because of missing config/credentials
- **MCP Failures**: Tools not available, servers not mounting, unexpected errors
- **Missing Capabilities**: Customer wants a feature that requires a service/API we haven't integrated yet
- **Subagent Issues**: Better coordination, new subagents needed, workflow improvements
- **New Tools/APIs**: Any tool or integration that would help you do your job better

### EXAMPLE RESPONSES

**WRONG** (never do this):
> "Please check your Render dashboard and verify the DATABASE_URL is set correctly."

**RIGHT** (do this instead):
> "I encountered a DATABASE_URL configuration issue. I've reported this to the Polsia team (report_platform_bug). Moving on to the parts I can complete. This will be fixed soon."

### KEY MINDSET

You're part of a team building autonomous infrastructure. If something is broken or missing, report it - **Polsia will build it**. Your job is to:
1. Do what you can
2. Report what's blocking you
3. Move on and explain what was skipped
4. Never leave the customer hanging or ask them to do ops work

## Bug Investigation Protocol

This is a **bug fix task**. You MUST investigate before implementing any fix.

### Step 1: Review Previous Attempts (if any)
If previous attempts are listed in the "Related Previous Work" section above:
1. Read the completion summaries — understand what was already tried
2. Run `git diff` on the files that were changed to see what fixes were attempted
3. Your fix MUST address something different from what was already tried

### Step 2: Investigate
1. Run `git log --oneline -20` to see what changed recently in this area
2. Run `git diff HEAD~5 -- path/to/suspect/files` to understand recent changes
3. Check for syntax errors: `node --check file.js` or `npx tsc --noEmit`

### Step 3: State Your Hypothesis
Before writing ANY fix, you MUST state:
- **Root cause:** [specific issue with evidence]
- **Evidence:** [error message, line number, or commit that introduced it]
- **Introduced by:** [commit hash if known]

DO NOT implement until you have a hypothesis with evidence.
If no evidence found, state what you checked and why you're uncertain.

### Step 4: Verify After Fix
1. Run linter — confirm no new syntax errors
2. Check Render logs — confirm no new errors appear
3. If the task included specific test steps, verify those work

DO NOT mark complete until the fix is verified working.

  "skill_name": "phased-task-execution",
  "summary": "Break complex engineering tasks into small phases that commit+deploy between each phase to avoid stuck/hung tasks and preserve progress",
  "content": "## When to Use
- ANY engineering task that touches more than 2-3 files
- ANY task that involves multiple distinct fixes or features
- ANY task that has gotten stuck before when attempted as one big unit
- Complex bug fixes with multiple symptoms (e.g., "TTS fails + mic loops + end button broken")
- Tasks for HireLoop (Rekrut AI) — owner expects phased delivery with QA between phases

## Prerequisites
- Task description clearly lists all issues/requirements
- Each phase should be completable in under 10 minutes of agent work

## Procedure

### 1. Split Into Phases BEFORE Starting Code
- Read the full task description
- Identify 2-4 independent phases (each phase = 1 deployable unit)
- Each phase MUST: commit, push, and deploy before moving to next
- Order phases by dependency (fix backend before frontend, fix data before UI)

### 2. Phase Execution Pattern
For EACH phase:
1. **Announce** what this phase will do (1 sentence)
2. **Code** the changes for THIS phase only — keep scope tight
3. **Test** the specific fix locally (curl, node check, etc.)
4. **Commit + Push + Deploy** — wait for deploy to confirm
5. **Log progress** — update task notes with what was completed
6. **Move to next phase** only after deploy succeeds

### 3. Phase Sizing Rules
- Each phase should touch MAX 3-5 files
- Each phase should take MAX 10 minutes
- If a phase feels too big → split it further
- Backend fixes and frontend fixes = separate phases
- API changes and UI changes = separate phases

### 4. Progress Checkpointing
- After each phase deploys, the work is SAFE even if agent crashes
- If task gets stuck mid-phase, only that one phase needs to be redone
- Task description should note which phases are done vs. remaining

### 5. Example Phase Breakdown
**Task:** "Mock Interview broken — TTS fails, mic loops, end button broken"
- Phase 1: Fix TTS fallback chain — ensure all providers are called in sequence with proper error handling
- Phase 2: Fix mic recording loop — stabilize STT capture and response cycle  
- Phase 3: Fix end button — force session cleanup and prevent fake feedback
- Phase 4: E2E test — run through full interview flow, verify all fixes work together

## Common Pitfalls
- **DON'T** try to fix everything in one giant commit — this is what causes stuck tasks
- **DON'T** skip the deploy step between phases — undeployed code = lost work if agent crashes
- **DON'T** combine backend + frontend in one phase — if one breaks, you lose both
- **DON'T** skip testing between phases — catching issues early prevents cascading failures
- **DO** keep phases independent so a failure in phase 3 doesn't undo phase 1-2
- **DO** commit with descriptive messages per phase (e.g., "Phase 1/4: Fix TTS fallback chain")

2. **Code** the changes for THIS phase only — keep scope tight
3. **Test** the specific fix locally (curl, node check, etc.)
4. **Commit + Push + Deploy** — wait for deploy to confirm
5. **Log progress** — update task notes with what was completed
6. **Move to next phase** only after deploy succeeds

### 3. Phase Sizing Rules
- Each phase should touch MAX 3-5 files
- Each phase should take MAX 10 minutes
- If a phase feels too big → split it further
- Backend fixes and frontend fixes = separate phases
- API changes and UI changes = separate phases

### 4. Progress Checkpointing
- After each phase deploys, the work is SAFE even if agent crashes
- If task gets stuck mid-phase, only that one phase needs to be redone
- Task description should note which phases are done vs. remaining

### 5. Example Phase Breakdown
**Task:** "Mock Interview broken — TTS fails, mic loops, end button broken"
- Phase 1: Fix TTS fallback chain — ensure all providers are called in sequence with proper error handling
- Phase 2: Fix mic recording loop — stabilize STT capture and response cycle  
- Phase 3: Fix end button — force session cleanup and prevent fake feedback
- Phase 4: E2E test — run through full interview flow, verify all fixes work together

## Common Pitfalls
- **DON'T** try to fix everything in one giant commit — this is what causes stuck tasks
- **DON'T** skip the deploy step between phases — undeployed code = lost work if agent crashes
- **DON'T** combine backend + frontend in one phase — if one breaks, you lose both
- **DON'T** skip testing between phases — catching issues early prevents cascading failures
- **DO** keep phases independent so a failure in phase 3 doesn't undo phase 1-2
- **DO** commit with descriptive messages per phase (e.g., "Phase 1/4: Fix TTS fallback chain")

## Summary: AI Call Patterns in HireLoop Codebase

Based on my thorough codebase analysis, here's a comprehensive report on the current AI call patterns and logging infrastructure.

### 1. **AI Call Architecture**

The app uses a **centralized, multi-provider fallback system** with two main AI handler files:

#### **`lib/polsia-ai.js`** (1,197 lines)
- **Entry point for all AI features** - wraps the AI provider service
- Provides high-level functions like:
  - `chat()` - Simple LLM calls with automatic provider fallback
  - `generateInterviewQuestions()` - Creates interview Q&A
  - `analyzeInterviewResponse()` - Evaluates candidate answers
  - `analyzeVideoPresentation()` - Video analysis with frame uploads
  - `analyzeVoiceQuality()` - Voice/speech analysis from transcripts
  - `transcribeAudioWithWhisper()` - Audio-to-text conversion
  - `textToSpeech()` - Speech synthesis with caching
  - `conductInterviewTurn()` - AI interviewer for mock interviews
  - `generateSessionFeedback()` - Post-interview analysis
- **All methods accept `subscriptionId` parameter** for per-user tracking
- Includes token budget awareness and logging hooks

#### **`lib/ai-provider.js`** (1,353 lines)
- **Comprehensive multi-provider fallback service** managing 7+ AI modalities:
  - LLM (chat completions) with 9-provider chain
  - Vision (video/image analysis) with 5-provider chain
  - TTS (text-to-speech) 
  - ASR (audio transcription)
  - Embeddings (vector generation)
  - Reranking (search result ordering)
  - Safety/moderation (content checking)

- **Provider chains by quality tier:**
  - **Default chain:** Anthropic → OpenAI → NIM Nemotron Super → NIM Kimi → NIM DeepSeek V3 → NIM Step Flash → NIM Qwen → NIM GPT-OSS
  - **Quality chain:** Same, but DeepSeek V3 prioritized after OpenAI
  - **Efficient chain:** Cheap models (Nano, Mistral, Nemotron Super)
  - **Reasoning chain:** Deep thinking models (DeepSeek R1, QwQ, Step Flash, etc.)

- **Circuit breaker pattern** - tracks failures per provider:modality, auto-recovery after 5 minutes
- **Token tracking** - integrates with `lib/token-budget.js` for usage monitoring
- **Environment variables used:**
  - `POLSIA_API_URL` / `POLSIA_API_KEY` - Polsia proxy endpoints
  - `OPENAI_BASE_URL` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` - OpenAI proxy
  - `NVIDIA_NIM_API_KEY` / `NIM_BASE_URL` - NVIDIA NIM fallbacks
  - 20+ NIM model overrides (environment configurable)

### 2. **Where AI Calls Happen**

**Direct AI Provider Usage (5 files):**
- `/lib/ai-provider.js` - Core service definition
- `/lib/polsia-ai.js` - High-level wrapper
- `/services/matching-engine.js` - Job candidate embeddings
- `/routes/interviews.js` - Interview Q&A generation & analysis
- `/routes/onboarding.js` - Onboarding chatbot (mentions polsiaAI)

**AI Functions Called from:**
- `/routes/interviews.js` - Interview experience (questions, analysis, coaching, video feedback, TTS)
- `/routes/candidate.js` - Resume parsing, skill assessments
- `/routes/assessments.js` - Dynamic assessment generation
- `/routes/recruiter.js` - Job optimization, candidate analysis
- `/routes/onboarding.js` - Onboarding assistant chatbot
- `/services/matching-engine.js` - Embeddings for job/candidate matching
- `/services/job-optimizer.js` - Job description enhancement
- `/services/document-verification.js` - Resume/document analysis

**No direct OpenAI/Anthropic calls** - All AI features route through the centralized providers.

### 3. **Existing Logging/Tracking Infrastructure**

#### **`lib/activity-logger.js`** (367 lines)
**Purpose:** Centralized event logging for the admin activity feed

**Two-tier approach:**
1. **In-memory buffer** (last 200 events) - for real-time dashboard feed
2. **Database persistence** (`activity_log` table) - for historical queries

**AI-specific logging functions:**
```javascript
logAICall(modality, provider, tokens, module, success)
  // Logs: ai_call_success / ai_call_failure
  // Details: { modality, provider, tokens, module }

logFailover(modality, fromProvider, toProvider, error)
  // Logs: ai_failover
  // Details: { modality, from, to, error }

logBudgetExhausted(tokensUsed, budget)
  // Logs: openai_budget_exhausted
  // Details: { tokensUsed, budget, message }
```

**Where it's hooked:**
- `/lib/ai-provider.js` line 486: Calls `activityLogger.logFailover()` on provider switch
- `/lib/ai-provider.js` line 499: Calls `tokenBudget.recordUsage()` which triggers budget logging
- NOT currently called for individual AI chat calls (opportunity for enhancement)

**Express middleware:** `requestLogger()` middleware logs all API requests, but only captures route patterns, not individual AI call details.

#### **`lib/token-budget.js`** (200+ lines)
**Purpose:** Track OpenAI token usage (100K/day default)

**Features:**
- Per-modality tracking (llm, tts, asr, vision, embedding, other)
- Per-provider tracking (openai vs nim)
- Daily reset at UTC midnight
- 7-day history
- Budget exhaustion triggers NIM-only routing
- Logs to activity feed when budget exhausted

### 4. **Current AI Call Patterns Analysis**

**CURRENT STATE: Mostly missing**
- AI provider calls happen in `aiProvider.chatCompletion()`, `aiProvider.visionAnalysis()`, etc.
- These calls are NOT currently being logged to the activity feed
- Token tracking happens only for OpenAI budget purposes, not for analytics
- No request-level logging of what prompt was sent or what response came back

**What IS being logged:**
- Provider failovers (automatic switches)
- Budget exhaustion events
- Generic API request routes (not AI-specific)

**What's NOT being logged:**
- Individual AI chat completions (no visibility into which module called chat, what the response was, latency)
- Vision analysis calls (video analysis, image processing)
- TTS/ASR calls (speech generation/transcription)
- Embedding calls (vector generation for matching)
- Per-call cost tracking
- Latency per provider
- Error details beyond circuit breaker metrics

### 5. **Module-Specific AI Usage**

The app defines module-specific provider chains in `/lib/ai-provider.js` lines 134-187:

| Module | Modality | Chain Type | Use Case |
|--------|----------|-----------|----------|
| mock-interview | llm, tts, asr, vision | quality | High-quality interview feedback |
| coaching | llm | quality | Coaching explanations |
| resume-parsing | llm, vision | default + document OCR | Resume extraction |
| job-matching | embedding, reranking | default + efficient LLM | Candidate-job matching |
| onboarding | llm | efficient | Simple Q&A for onboarding |
| assessments | llm | reasoning | Complex skill evaluations |
| payroll | llm | efficient | Payroll calculations |

### 6. **Key Files for AI Call Logging**

**Files to monitor for comprehensive logging:**
1. `/lib/ai-provider.js` (1353 lines) - Core execution point
   - `execute()` method (line 554) - All AI calls flow through here
   - `chatCompletion()` (line 628) - LLM calls
   - `visionAnalysis()` (line 757) - Video/image analysis
   - `textToSpeech()` (line 834) - TTS calls
   - `transcribeAudio()` (line 1028) - ASR calls
   - `generateEmbedding()` (line 1119) - Embeddings
   - `moderateContent()` (line 1244) - Safety checks
   
2. `/lib/activity-logger.js` (367 lines) - Logging infrastructure
   - Functions: `logAICall()`, `logFailover()`, `logBudgetExhausted()`
   - Route event mapping (lines 173-242)

3. `/lib/polsia-ai.js` (1197 lines) - High-level AI functions
   - Every function wraps `aiProvider` calls
   - All accept `subscriptionId` for per-user tracking

### 7. **Environment Variables Controlling AI**

| Variable | Purpose | Default |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `POLSIA_API_URL` | Polsia proxy base URL | `https://polsia.com/api/proxy/ai` |
| `POLSIA_API_KEY` | Authentication for Polsia | (required) |
| `OPENAI_BASE_URL` | OpenAI proxy URL | (required if using OpenAI) |
| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI API key | (required if using OpenAI) |
| `NVIDIA_NIM_API_KEY` | NVIDIA NIM API key | (optional for fallbacks) |
| `NIM_BASE_URL` | NVIDIA NIM base URL | `https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1` |
| `OPENAI_DAILY_TOKEN_BUDGET` | Daily token limit | `100000` |
| 20+ `NIM_LLM_*`, `NIM_VISION_*` | Model selection overrides | Various defaults |

### 8. **Agent SDK Skill Reference**

**From `.claude/skills/agent-sdk/SKILL.md`:**
- HireLoop uses the **Polsia AI proxy pattern** (documented in skill)
- This is different - the skill shows client apps should call Polsia API directly
- HireLoop (being a Polsia internal app) uses a different pattern: direct SDK calls with automatic provider fallback
- The skill documents subscription tracking via `X-Subscription-ID` header - this is implemented in `lib/ai-provider.js` for Anthropic calls

### 9. **Recommendations for Comprehensive Logging**

To build comprehensive AI call logging, you should:

1. **Instrument `aiProvider.execute()`** (line 554) - Wrap with start/end timing, log call signature
2. **Add call details to `logAICall()`** - Include: module, input tokens, output tokens, latency, response preview
3. **Hook into each provider method** - Track which provider actually served the request
4. **Create AI call catalog** - Map module + function + modality to readable events
5. **Database schema** - Extend `activity_log` or create separate `ai_calls` table with indexed queries
6. **Budget attribution** - Link cost to module, user subscription, date for chargeback

The infrastructure is **95% there** - you just need to add the actual logging statements at the execution points.

   375→  /**

   379→  getModuleChain(module, modality) {

   397→      case 'nim_nemotron_super':

   406→      case 'nim_deepseek_r1':
   407→      case 'nim_qwq':
   408→      case 'nim_cosmos':
   409→      case 'nim_nemotron_vl':
   410→      case 'nim_kimi_vision':
   411→      case 'nim_gemma_vision':
   412→      case 'nim_riva_tts':
   413→      case 'nim_magpie_multilingual':
   414→      case 'nim_magpie_flow':

   431→  // ─── Circuit Breaker ──────────────────────────────────────────
   432→
   433→  isCircuitOpen(providerKey, modality) {
   434→    const key = `${providerKey}:${modality}`;
   435→    const failure = this.failures[key];
   436→    if (!failure) return false;
   437→
   438→    const elapsed = Date.now() - failure.lastFailure;
   439→    if (elapsed > CIRCUIT_BREAK_MS) {
   440→      // Circuit closed — enough time passed, allow retry
   441→      delete this.failures[key];
   442→      return false;
   443→    }
   444→    return true;
   445→  }
   446→
   447→  recordFailure(providerKey, modality, error) {
   448→    const key = `${providerKey}:${modality}`;
   449→    const existing = this.failures[key] || { count: 0 };
   450→    const status = error.status || error.statusCode || null;
   451→
   452→    this.failures[key] = {
   453→      count: existing.count + 1,
   454→      lastFailure: Date.now(),
   455→      error: error.message || String(error),
   456→      status,
   457→    };
   458→
   459→    this.stats.totalFailovers++;
   460→
   461→    const logEntry = {
   462→      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
   463→      event: 'provider_failure',
   464→      provider: providerKey,
   465→      modality,
   466→      error: error.message || String(error),
   467→      status,
   468→    };
   469→    this.logs.push(logEntry);
   470→    if (this.logs.length > 100) this.logs.shift();
   471→
   472→    console.log(`[ai-provider] ❌ ${providerKey} failed for ${modality}: [${status || 'ERR'}] ${error.message || error}`);
   473→  }
   474→
   475→  _logFailover(fromProvider, toProvider, modality) {
   476→    const logEntry = {
   477→      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
   478→      event: 'failover',
   479→      from: fromProvider,
   480→      to: toProvider,
   481→      modality,
   482→    };
   483→    this.logs.push(logEntry);
   484→    if (this.logs.length > 100) this.logs.shift();
   485→    console.log(`[ai-provider] 🔄 ${modality} failover: ${fromProvider} → ${toProvider}`);
   486→    activityLogger.logFailover(modality, fromProvider, toProvider);
   487→  }
   488→
   489→  recordSuccess(providerKey, modality, tokenCount = 0) {
   490→    this.activeProviders[modality] = providerKey;
   491→    const key = `${providerKey}:${modality}`;
   492→    delete this.failures[key];
   493→
   494→    this.stats.totalCalls++;
   495→    this.stats.providerCalls[providerKey] = (this.stats.providerCalls[providerKey] || 0) + 1;
   496→
   497→    // Track OpenAI token usage for budget enforcement
   498→    if (this._isOpenAIProvider(providerKey) && tokenCount > 0) {
   499→      tokenBudget.recordUsage(modality, tokenCount);
   500→    }
   501→  }
   502→
   503→  isRetryable(err) {
   504→    // Network/timeout errors
   505→    if (err.code === 'ETIMEDOUT' || err.code === 'ECONNREFUSED' || err.code === 'ECONNRESET' || err.code === 'ENOTFOUND') return true;
   506→    if (err.message && (err.message.includes('timeout') || err.message.includes('ETIMEDOUT'))) return true;
   507→
   508→    // HTTP status codes
   509→    const status = err.status || err.statusCode || (err.response && err.response.status);
   510→    if (status && RETRYABLE_STATUS.has(status)) return true;
   511→
   512→    // OpenAI SDK-specific error types
   513→    if (err.type === 'insufficient_quota' || err.code === 'insufficient_quota') return true;
   514→    if (err.type === 'rate_limit_exceeded' || err.code === 'rate_limit_exceeded') return true;
   515→
   516→    // Anthropic-specific
   517→    if (err.error && err.error.type === 'overloaded_error') return true;
   518→
   519→    return false;
   520→  }
   521→
   522→  // Check if a provider key is an OpenAI provider (not Anthropic, not NIM)
   523→  _isOpenAIProvider(providerKey) {
   524→    return providerKey === 'openai' || providerKey.startsWith('openai_');
   525→  }
   526→
   527→  // Get available providers for a modality (skipping unavailable + circuit-broken + budget-exhausted OpenAI)
   528→  getAvailableChain(modality, variant = 'default') {
   529→    const budgetExhausted = tokenBudget.isOpenAIBudgetExhausted();
   530→    // TTS and ASR are exempt from budget exhaustion — they use minimal tokens
   531→    // (~50-100 per TTS call, ~30-50 per ASR call) and have NO working NIM fallbacks
   532→    // (NVIDIA cloud API doesn't host audio models). Blocking them kills interviews.
   533→    const budgetExemptModalities = new Set(['tts', 'asr']);
   534→    const skipOpenAI = budgetExhausted && !budgetExemptModalities.has(modality);
   535→
   536→    return this.getChain(modality, variant).filter(key => {
   537→      if (!this.isProviderAvailable(key)) return false;
   538→      if (this.isCircuitOpen(key, modality)) return false;
   539→      // Skip OpenAI providers when daily token budget is exhausted (except TTS/ASR)
   540→      if (skipOpenAI && this._isOpenAIProvider(key)) return false;
   541→      return true;
   542→    });
   543→  }
   544→
   545→  // ─── Core Execution with Fallback ────────────────────────────
   546→
   547→  /**
   548→   * Execute an AI call with automatic fallback.
   549→   * @param {string} modality - 'llm', 'vision', 'tts', 'asr', 'embedding', 'reranking', 'safety'
   550→   * @param {Function} executeFn - async (providerKey) => result
   551→   * @param {string} variant - Chain variant (default, quality, efficient, reasoning)
   552→   * @returns {*} Result from the first successful provider
   553→   */
   554→  async execute(modality, executeFn, variant = 'default') {
   555→    const chain = this.getAvailableChain(modality, variant);
   556→
   557→    if (chain.length === 0) {
   558→      const allChain = this.getChain(modality, variant);
   559→      const reasons = allChain.map(k => {
   560→        if (!this.isProviderAvailable(k)) return `${k}: not configured`;
   561→        if (this.isCircuitOpen(k, modality)) return `${k}: circuit open`;
   562→        return `${k}: unknown`;
   563→      });
   564→      throw new Error(`No available providers for ${modality}. ${reasons.join(', ')}`);
   565→    }
   566→
   567→    // Per-provider timeout: 8s for TTS/ASR (audio processing), 15s for LLM, 10s default
   568→    const timeoutMs = modality === 'tts' || modality === 'asr' ? 8000
   569→      : modality === 'llm' ? 15000
   570→      : 10000;
   571→
   572→    let lastError;
   573→    let failedProvider = null;  // Track from→to for failover logging
   574→    for (const providerKey of chain) {
   575→      try {
   576→        // Wrap each provider call with a timeout to prevent hanging
   577→        const result = await Promise.race([
   578→          executeFn(providerKey),
   579→          new Promise((_, reject) => setTimeout(() => {
   580→            const err = new Error(`${providerKey} timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms for ${modality}`);
   581→            err.code = 'ETIMEDOUT';
   582→            reject(err);
   583→          }, timeoutMs))
   584→        ]);
   585→        // Log successful failover transition if we had a prior failure
   586→        if (failedProvider) {
   587→          this._logFailover(failedProvider, providerKey, modality);
   588→        }
   589→        // Extract token count if result is wrapped with _tokenCount
   590→        let tokenCount = 0;
   591→        let actualResult = result;
   592→        if (result && typeof result === 'object' && result._tokenCount !== undefined) {
   593→          tokenCount = result._tokenCount;
   594→          actualResult = result._result;
   595→        }
   596→        this.recordSuccess(providerKey, modality, tokenCount);
   597→        return actualResult;
   598→      } catch (err) {
   599→        lastError = err;
   600→        // Provider not applicable for this request (e.g., voice cloning TTS without audio prompt)
   601→        // Skip silently without circuit-breaking — provider is fine, just wrong for this request
   602→        if (err.notApplicable) {
   603→          continue;
   604→        }
   605→        if (this.isRetryable(err)) {
   606→          failedProvider = providerKey;
   607→          this.recordFailure(providerKey, modality, err);
   608→          continue; // try next provider
   609→        }
   610→        // Non-retryable error (e.g., bad request, invalid input) — don't try others
   611→        throw err;
   612→      }
   613→    }
   614→
   615→    // All providers exhausted
   616→    console.error(`[ai-provider] All ${modality} providers exhausted.`);
   617→    throw lastError || new Error(`All ${modality} providers failed`);
   618→  }

   667→  async _anthropicChat(messages, options) {
   668→    const systemMsgs = messages.filter(m => m.role === 'system');
   669→    const nonSystemMsgs = messages.filter(m => m.role !== 'system');
   670→
   671→    const systemPrompt = options.system || (systemMsgs.length > 0 ? systemMsgs[0].content : undefined);
   672→    const msgs = nonSystemMsgs.map(m => ({
   673→      role: m.role,
   674→      content: typeof m.content === 'string' ? m.content : String(m.content),
   675→    }));
   676→    if (msgs.length === 0) msgs.push({ role: 'user', content: 'Hello' });
   677→
   678→    const response = await this.clients.anthropic.messages.create({
   679→      max_tokens: options.maxTokens || options.max_tokens || 8192,
   680→      messages: msgs,
   681→      system: systemPrompt,
   682→    }, {
   683→      headers: options.subscriptionId ? { 'X-Subscription-ID': options.subscriptionId } : {},
   684→    });
   685→
   686→    return response.content[0].text;
   687→  }

   689→  async _openaiChat(model, messages, options, isPolsia = false) {
   690→    const client = this.clients.openai;
   691→    const msgs = [];
   692→
   693→    if (options.system) {
   694→      msgs.push({ role: 'system', content: options.system });
   695→    }
   696→    for (const m of messages) {
   697→      if (m.role === 'system' && options.system) continue; // already added
   698→      msgs.push({ role: m.role, content: typeof m.content === 'string' ? m.content : String(m.content) });
   699→    }
   700→    if (msgs.length === 0) msgs.push({ role: 'user', content: 'Hello' });
   701→
   702→    const params = {
   703→      model,
   704→      messages: msgs,
   705→      max_tokens: options.maxTokens || options.max_tokens || 8192,
   706→      temperature: options.temperature || 0.7,
   707→    };
   708→
   709→    // Polsia proxy requires task field
   710→    if (isPolsia) params.task = 'chat-fallback';
   711→    if (options.response_format) params.response_format = options.response_format;
   712→
   713→    const response = await client.chat.completions.create(params);
   714→    const text = response.choices[0].message.content;
   715→    // Track token usage from response or estimate
   716→    const tokenCount = (response.usage && response.usage.total_tokens)
   717→      ? response.usage.total_tokens
   718→      : tokenBudget.estimateTokensFromText(text) + tokenBudget.estimateTokensFromText(msgs.map(m => m.content).join(' '));
   719→    return { _result: text, _tokenCount: tokenCount };
   720→  }

   861→  async _openaiTTS(text, options) {

   865→
   866→    const baseUrl = this.openaiBaseUrl || 'https://api.openai.com/v1';

   869→    const headers = {
   870→      'Authorization': `Bearer ${apiKey}`,

   872→    };

   875→    const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/audio/speech`, {
   876→      method: 'POST',

   878→      body: JSON.stringify({
   879→        model: 'tts-1',
   880→        input: truncatedText,
   881→        voice,
   882→        response_format: 'mp3',
   883→        speed,
   884→        task: 'tts-interview',
   885→      }),
   886→    });
   887→
   888→    if (!res.ok) {

   895→    const arrayBuffer = await res.arrayBuffer();

   944→  /**
   945→   * NIM Magpie/FastPitch TTS — Generic adapter for all NIM TTS models.
   946→   * Uses OpenAI-compatible /v1/audio/speech endpoint with model parameter.
   947→   * Voice-cloning models (Flow, Zeroshot) require an audio prompt — if not provided,
   948→   * they throw a notApplicable error and the fallback system skips them silently.
   949→   * @param {string} configKey - Key into NIM_TTS_CONFIGS
   950→   * @param {string} text - Text to synthesize
   951→   * @param {Object} options - { language, nimVoice, audioPrompt, audioPromptTranscript }

   954→  async _nimMagpieTTS(configKey, text, options) {

   957→
   958→    // Voice-cloning models require audio prompts — skip gracefully if not provided
   959→    if (config.requiresAudioPrompt && !options.audioPrompt) {
   960→      const err = new Error(`${config.name} requires an audio prompt for voice cloning — skipping`);
   961→      err.notApplicable = true;
   962→      throw err;
   963→    }
   964→
   965→    const truncatedText = text.length > (config.maxTextLength || 4000)
   966→      ? text.substring(0, config.maxTextLength || 4000)
   967→      : text;
   968→
   969→    // Map config keys to NIM model IDs
   970→    const modelMap = {
   971→      nim_magpie_multilingual: NIM_MODELS.tts_magpie_multilingual,
   972→      nim_magpie_flow: NIM_MODELS.tts_magpie_flow,
   973→      nim_magpie_zeroshot: NIM_MODELS.tts_magpie_zeroshot,
   974→      nim_fastpitch: NIM_MODELS.tts_fastpitch,
   975→    };
   976→    const model = modelMap[configKey];
   977→    if (!model) throw new Error(`No NIM model ID for TTS config: ${configKey}`);
   978→
   979→    // Use model-specific base URL if configured, otherwise default NIM base
   980→    const baseUrl = config.baseUrl || process.env.NIM_TTS_BASE_URL || this.nimBaseUrl;
   981→
   982→    // Use OpenAI-compatible /v1/audio/speech endpoint (JSON body)
   983→    const voice = config.defaultVoice || options.nimVoice || 'default';
   984→
   985→    const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/audio/speech`, {
   986→      method: 'POST',
   987→      headers: {
   988→        'Authorization': `Bearer ${this.nimApiKey}`,
   989→        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
   990→        'Accept': 'audio/wav',
   991→      },
   992→      body: JSON.stringify({
   993→        model,
   994→        input: truncatedText,
   995→        voice,
   996→        response_format: 'wav',
   997→      }),
   998→    });
   999→
  1000→    if (!res.ok) {
  1001→      const errText = await res.text();
  1002→      const err = new Error(`NIM ${config.name} error: ${res.status} ${errText.substring(0, 200)}`);
  1003→      err.status = res.status;
  1004→      throw err;
  1005→    }
  1006→
  1007→    const arrayBuffer = await res.arrayBuffer();
  1008→    const buffer = Buffer.from(arrayBuffer);
  1009→
  1010→    if (buffer.length < 100) {
  1011→      throw new Error(`NIM ${config.name} returned empty/invalid audio`);
  1012→    }
  1013→
  1014→    return buffer;
  1015→  }

  1019→  /**

  1048→  async _openaiWhisper(audioBuffer, filename, contentType, options) {
  1049→    const formData = new FormData();
  1050→    formData.append('file', audioBuffer, { filename, contentType });
  1051→    formData.append('model', 'whisper-1');
  1052→    formData.append('response_format', 'verbose_json');
  1053→
  1054→    const baseUrl = this.openaiBaseUrl || 'https://api.openai.com/v1';
  1055→    const apiKey = this.openaiApiKey;
  1056→
  1057→    const headers = {
  1058→      'Authorization': `Bearer ${apiKey}`,
  1059→      ...formData.getHeaders(),
  1060→    };
  1061→    if (options.subscriptionId) headers['X-Subscription-ID'] = options.subscriptionId;
  1062→
  1063→    const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/audio/transcriptions`, {
  1064→      method: 'POST',
  1065→      headers,
  1066→      body: formData,
  1067→    });
  1068→
  1069→    if (!res.ok) {
  1070→      const errText = await res.text();
  1071→      const err = new Error(`Whisper API error: ${res.status} ${errText.substring(0, 200)}`);
  1072→      err.status = res.status;
  1073→      throw err;
  1074→    }
  1075→
  1076→    const result = await res.json();
  1077→    // Track ASR token usage based on audio buffer size
  1078→    const asrTokens = tokenBudget.estimateASRTokens(audioBuffer.length);
  1079→    return { _result: result, _tokenCount: asrTokens };
  1080→  }
  1081→
  1082→  async _nimASR(model, audioBuffer, filename, contentType, options) {
  1083→    // NIM Parakeet uses OpenAI-compatible transcription endpoint
  1084→    const formData = new FormData();
  1085→    formData.append('file', audioBuffer, { filename, contentType });
  1086→    formData.append('model', model);
  1087→    formData.append('response_format', 'verbose_json');
  1088→
  1089→    const headers = {
  1090→      'Authorization': `Bearer ${this.nimApiKey}`,
  1091→      ...formData.getHeaders(),
  1092→    };
  1093→
  1094→    const res = await fetch(`${this.nimBaseUrl}/audio/transcriptions`, {
  1095→      method: 'POST',
  1096→      headers,
  1097→      body: formData,
  1098→    });
  1099→
  1100→    if (!res.ok) {
  1101→      const errText = await res.text();
  1102→      const err = new Error(`NIM ASR (${model}) error: ${res.status} ${errText.substring(0, 200)}`);
  1103→      err.status = res.status;
  1104→      throw err;
  1105→    }
  1106→
  1107→    return await res.json();
  1108→  }
  1109→
  1110→  // ─── Embeddings ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  1111→
  1112→  /**
  1113→   * Generate text embeddings with automatic provider fallback.
  1114→   * Chain: OpenAI text-embedding-3-small → NIM NV-EmbedQA → NIM Nemotron-Embed-VL
  1115→   * @param {string} text - Text to embed
  1116→   * @param {Object} options - { subscriptionId }
  1117→   * @returns {number[]} Embedding vector
  1118→   */
  1119→  async generateEmbedding(text, options = {}) {
  1120→    if (!text || text.trim().length === 0) {
  1121→      throw new Error('Cannot generate embedding for empty text');
  1122→    }
  1123→
  1124→    return this.execute('embedding', async (providerKey) => {
  1125→      switch (providerKey) {
  1126→        case 'openai_embed':
  1127→          return this._openaiEmbedding(text, options);
  1128→        case 'nim_embed_qa':
  1129→          return this._nimEmbedding(NIM_MODELS.embed_qa, text, options);
  1130→        case 'nim_embed_vl':
  1131→          return this._nimEmbedding(NIM_MODELS.embed_vl, text, options);
  1132→        default:
  1133→          throw new Error(`Unknown embedding provider: ${providerKey}`);
  1134→      }
  1135→    });
  1136→  }
  1137→
  1138→  async _openaiEmbedding(text, options) {
  1139→    const client = this.clients.openai;
  1140→    const truncated = text.substring(0, 8000);
  1141→
  1142→    const params = {
  1143→      model: 'text-embedding-3-small',
  1144→      input: truncated,
  1145→    };
  1146→    if (options.subscriptionId) params.task = 'embedding';
  1147→
  1148→    const response = await client.embeddings.create(params);
  1149→    const embedding = response.data[0].embedding;
  1150→    // Track embedding token usage
  1151→    const embedTokens = (response.usage && response.usage.total_tokens)
  1152→      ? response.usage.total_tokens
  1153→      : tokenBudget.estimateTokensFromText(truncated);
  1154→    return { _result: embedding, _tokenCount: embedTokens };
  1155→  }
  1156→
  1157→  async _nimEmbedding(model, text, options) {
  1158→    const client = this.clients.nim;
  1159→    const truncated = text.substring(0, 8000);
  1160→
  1161→    const response = await client.embeddings.create({
  1162→      model,
  1163→      input: truncated,
  1164→    });
  1165→    return response.data[0].embedding;
  1166→  }
  1167→
  1168→  // ─── Reranking ───────────────────────────────────────────────
  1169→
  1170→  /**
  1171→   * Rerank search results for quality improvement.
  1172→   * Uses NIM NV-RerankQA model to reorder candidate/job matches.
  1173→   * @param {string} query - The search query
  1174→   * @param {string[]} documents - Array of document texts to rerank
  1175→   * @param {Object} options - { topN }
  1176→   * @returns {Array} Reranked results with scores
  1177→   */
  1178→  async rerank(query, documents, options = {}) {
  1179→    if (!this.nimAvailable) {
  1180→      // No NIM = return original order with default scores
  1181→      console.log('[ai-provider] Reranking skipped (NIM not available), returning original order');
  1182→      return documents.map((doc, i) => ({ index: i, score: 1 - (i * 0.01), text: doc }));
  1183→    }
  1184→
  1185→    try {
  1186→      return await this.execute('reranking', async (providerKey) => {
  1187→        let model;
  1188→        switch (providerKey) {
  1189→          case 'nim_rerank_qa':
  1190→            model = NIM_MODELS.rerank_qa;
  1191→            break;
  1192→          case 'nim_rerank_vl':
  1193→            model = NIM_MODELS.rerank_vl;
  1194→            break;
  1195→          default:
  1196→            throw new Error(`Unknown reranking provider: ${providerKey}`);
  1197→        }
  1198→
  1199→        const topN = options.topN || documents.length;
  1200→
  1201→        // NIM reranking endpoint
  1202→        const res = await fetch(`${this.nimBaseUrl}/ranking`, {
  1203→          method: 'POST',
  1204→          headers: {
  1205→            'Authorization': `Bearer ${this.nimApiKey}`,
  1206→            'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  1207→          },
  1208→          body: JSON.stringify({
  1209→            model,
  1210→            query: { text: query },
  1211→            passages: documents.map(doc => ({ text: doc })),
  1212→            top_n: topN,
  1213→          }),
  1214→        });
  1215→
  1216→        if (!res.ok) {
  1217→          const errText = await res.text();
  1218→          const err = new Error(`NIM Reranking error: ${res.status} ${errText.substring(0, 200)}`);
  1219→          err.status = res.status;
  1220→          throw err;
  1221→        }
  1222→
  1223→        const result = await res.json();
  1224→        return (result.rankings || []).map(r => ({
  1225→          index: r.index,
  1226→          score: r.logit || r.score || 0,
  1227→          text: documents[r.index],
  1228→        }));
  1229→      });
  1230→    } catch (err) {
  1231→      console.error('[ai-provider] Reranking failed, returning original order:', err.message);
  1232→      return documents.map((doc, i) => ({ index: i, score: 1 - (i * 0.01), text: doc }));
  1233→    }
  1234→  }
  1235→
  1236→  // ─── Safety / Content Moderation ─────────────────────────────
  1237→
  1238→  /**
  1239→   * Check content for safety violations using NIM NemoGuard.
  1240→   * @param {string} content - Text to check
  1241→   * @param {Object} options - { categories }
  1242→   * @returns {Object} { safe: boolean, categories: {...}, reason: string }
  1243→   */
  1244→  async moderateContent(content, options = {}) {
  1245→    if (!this.nimAvailable) {
  1246→      // No NIM = allow all (no moderation available)
  1247→      return { safe: true, categories: {}, reason: 'Moderation not available (NIM not configured)' };
  1248→    }
  1249→
  1250→    try {
  1251→      return await this.execute('safety', async (providerKey) => {
  1252→        let model;
  1253→        switch (providerKey) {
  1254→          case 'nim_safety_guard':
  1255→            model = NIM_MODELS.safety_guard;
  1256→            break;
  1257→          case 'nim_safety_reasoning':
  1258→            model = NIM_MODELS.safety_reasoning;
  1259→            break;
  1260→          default:
  1261→            throw new Error(`Unknown safety provider: ${providerKey}`);
  1262→        }
  1263→
  1264→        // NemoGuard uses chat completions format with safety-specific system prompt
  1265→        const response = await this.clients.nim.chat.completions.create({
  1266→          model,
  1267→          messages: [
  1268→            {
  1269→              role: 'system',
  1270→              content: 'You are a content safety classifier. Analyze the following text for safety violations including: harassment, hate speech, violence, sexual content, self-harm, dangerous content, and professional misconduct. Return a JSON object: {"safe": true/false, "categories": {"harassment": false, "hate_speech": false, "violence": false, "sexual_content": false, "self_harm": false, "dangerous": false, "unprofessional": false}, "reason": "explanation if unsafe"}'
  1271→            },
  1272→            { role: 'user', content: content.substring(0, 4000) }
  1273→          ],
  1274→          max_tokens: 512,
  1275→          temperature: 0.1,
  1276→        });
  1277→
  1278→        const text = response.choices[0].message.content;
  1279→        try {
  1280→          const jsonMatch = text.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/);
  1281→          if (jsonMatch) return JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]);
  1282→        } catch (e) { /* fall through */ }
  1283→
  1284→        // Default safe if parsing fails
  1285→        return { safe: true, categories: {}, reason: 'Safety check completed (parse fallback)' };
  1286→      });
  1287→    } catch (err) {
  1288→      console.error('[ai-provider] Safety moderation failed:', err.message);
  1289→      return { safe: true, categories: {}, reason: 'Safety check failed (allowing by default)' };
  1290→    }
  1291→  }
  1292→
  1293→  // ─── Health Status ────────────────────────────────────────────
  1294→
  1295→  getHealth() {
  1296→    const allModalities = ['llm', 'vision', 'tts', 'asr', 'embedding', 'reranking', 'safety'];
  1297→    const modalities = {};
  1298→
  1299→    for (const modality of allModalities) {
  1300→      const chain = this.getChain(modality);
  1301→      modalities[modality] = {
  1302→        active: this.activeProviders[modality] || 'none',
  1303→        chain_depth: chain.length,
  1304→        providers: chain.map(key => ({
  1305→          key,
  1306→          available: this.isProviderAvailable(key),
  1307→          circuitOpen: this.isCircuitOpen(key, modality),
  1308→          failures: this.failures[`${key}:${modality}`] || null,
  1309→        })),
  1310→      };
  1311→    }
  1312→
  1313→    // Module-specific chain info
  1314→    const moduleInfo = {};
  1315→    for (const [module, config] of Object.entries(MODULE_CHAINS)) {
  1316→      moduleInfo[module] = {};
  1317→      for (const [modality, variant] of Object.entries(config)) {
  1318→        const chain = this.getChain(modality, variant);
  1319→        const available = chain.filter(k => this.isProviderAvailable(k) && !this.isCircuitOpen(k, modality));
  1320→        moduleInfo[module][modality] = {
  1321→          variant,
  1322→          chain_depth: chain.length,
  1323→          available_count: available.length,
  1324→          providers: chain.map(k => k),
  1325→        };
  1326→      }
  1327→    }
  1328→
  1329→    return {
  1330→      status: 'ok',
  1331→      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
  1332→      nim_configured: this.nimAvailable,
  1333→      total_models_registered: Object.keys(NIM_MODELS).length,
  1334→      stats: this.stats,
  1335→      token_budget: tokenBudget.getStatus(),
  1336→      modalities,
  1337→      module_chains: moduleInfo,
  1338→      recent_logs: this.logs.slice(-20),
  1339→    };
  1340→  }

/**
 * Migration 039: AI Health Monitoring — Call Logs, Prompt Registry, Model Metrics
 *
 * Creates tables for:
 * 1. ai_call_log — Every AI call with full metadata (latency, tokens, cost, module)
 * 2. ai_prompts — Prompt registry with version control
 * 3. ai_prompt_versions — Version history for each prompt
 * 4. ai_ab_tests — A/B test configurations for prompts
 */

module.exports = {
  name: '039_ai_health_monitoring',
  async up(client) {
    // ─── AI Call Log — comprehensive per-call tracking ───
    await client.query(`
      CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ai_call_log (
        id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
        module VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
        feature VARCHAR(200),
        modality VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
        provider VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
        model VARCHAR(200),
        prompt_tokens INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
        completion_tokens INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
        total_tokens INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
        latency_ms INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
        success BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
        error_message TEXT,
        cost_estimate DECIMAL(10, 6) DEFAULT 0,
        fallback_chain JSONB,
        user_id INTEGER,
        prompt_id INTEGER,
        prompt_version INTEGER,
        created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
      )
    `);

    await client.query(`CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ai_call_log_created ON ai_call_log (created_at)`);
    await client.query(`CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ai_call_log_module ON ai_call_log (module)`);
    await client.query(`CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ai_call_log_provider ON ai_call_log (provider)`);
    await client.query(`CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ai_call_log_modality ON ai_call_log (modality)`);
    await client.query(`CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ai_call_log_success ON ai_call_log (success)`);

    // ─── AI Prompts — central registry ───
    await client.query(`
      CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ai_prompts (
        id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
        slug VARCHAR(200) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
        name VARCHAR(300) NOT NULL,
        module VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
        feature VARCHAR(200),
        description TEXT,
        current_version INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
        model VARCHAR(200),
        avg_tokens DECIMAL(10, 1) DEFAULT 0,
        avg_latency_ms DECIMAL(10, 1) DEFAULT 0,
        success_rate DECIMAL(5, 2) DEFAULT 100,
        total_calls INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
        is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
        created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
        updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
      )
    `);

    // ─── AI Prompt Versions — version history ───
    await client.query(`
      CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ai_prompt_versions (
        id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
        prompt_id INTEGER REFERENCES ai_prompts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
        version INTEGER NOT NULL,
        system_prompt TEXT,
        user_template TEXT,
        temperature DECIMAL(3, 2) DEFAULT 0.7,
        max_tokens INTEGER DEFAULT 8192,
